checklist.details: All 7 ACs have corresponding test cases
Test fails because target file does not exist (implementation missing), not due to syntax/import errors
bash -n confirms script syntax is valid
Clear PASS/FAIL messages, tests validate content patterns not just file existence
Documentation tests use appropriate pattern matching (grep) for semantic validation
Tests examine only public artifact (INTERVIEW-PATTERN.md) via file content inspection

checklist.item: No implementation code beyond minimal stubs
checklist.details: No implementation artifact created, only test script

AC-2.criterion: Documents five-phase flow with clear phase boundaries
tests: Lines 21-29: Presence of all five phases (GATHER, ASSESS, INTERVIEW, SYNTHESIZE, PRODUCE)
Lines 32-38: Phase boundary clarity via heading count
Lines 41-49: Three context mechanisms (territory, memory, context-explorer)
Lines 77-89: Depth tiers (small, medium, large) documented

AC-5.criterion: Specifies error handling patterns for context failures

AC-6.criterion: Includes yield context enrichment format with gap analysis metadata
tests: Lines 142-154: Gap analysis fields (total_key_questions, questions_answered, coverage_percent)

AC-7.criterion: Documents when to yield blocked, need-decision, or continue with partial context

strengths: Comprehensive coverage of all 7 acceptance criteria
Clear PASS/FAIL output makes test failures immediately actionable
Uses appropriate testing patterns: grep for semantic content validation, not just structure
Tests validate behavior (presence of concepts) not just file existence
Flexible pattern matching (e.g., 'coverage_percent|coverage.*percent') handles formatting variations
Tests exit immediately on first failure with clear diagnostic
Set -e ensures test script fails fast on any error
No hardcoded expectations for exact wording, allows for documentation style flexibility

test_failure_verification.notes: Test fails because implementation artifact is missing, not due to syntax/import errors. This is the correct red phase failure.
